$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH. scripts/build-handoff.mjs generates HANDOFF.md, handoff.html, and handover/*.json from this — never hand-edit the generated files. Direction documented: Playful tall-scene (the chosen, locked direction). Deep-links point at the version-0.2 prototype. Copy is verbatim from the prototype.
title: Playground Sign-in
summary: The Playground educator sign-in journey for a SHARED ROOM TABLET: service login → educator select → PIN → room select → room hub, plus the returning-educator (same-day) path. The chosen, locked direction is Playful tall-scene: a full-bleed teal hero (kid-friendly drifting scene) over a white sheet, on real Stardust --sd-* tokens. The hero brand mark swaps with progress — Playground 'P' (service) → the service's logo, e.g. Little Bugs (educator select) → the signing-in educator's photo (PIN / room / same-day). Because the device is shared, identity safety is first-class: Switch educator and Log out are always reachable, and the session idle-locks after ~30s on the hub (PIN re-auth). 'Done' = a returning educator reaches their room hub in under four taps, no error state dead-ends, and a tablet left unattended cannot be acted on as the signed-in educator. Exact pixels live in the runnable prototype — each screen links to its live state (append &bare=1 for a chrome-free frame).
outOfScope: Room Hub screen itself (Phase 3 — separate spec); this flow ends by routing into it
Offline sync + token-refresh internals (handled by the offline architecture, Phase 1)
Final educator photos / service logos — the prototype uses swappable placeholder images (pravatar / loremflickr / a 'Little Bugs' crest)

components.name: ds-input
components.notes: 52px tall, radius-lg, 1px grey-500 border; leading-icon (username) + trailing password-reveal toggle slots

components.name: ds-btn
components.notes: primary full-width 52px; loading state shows spinner + 'Signing in…'; outline used for Change room / Switch educator

components.name: ds-card
components.notes: educator rows (photo + name + last-login) and room rows (photo thumbnail + ratio badge + selected/green, needs-attention amber accent, disabled/closed)

components.name: ds-avatar
components.notes: educator avatars are photo-first with a graceful initials fallback if the image fails to load

components.name: ds-message-box
components.notes: inline Alert banner (offline / closed-room) with optional action link

components.name: ds-pin-keypad
components.notes: NET-NEW: 4 PIN dots (ring→filled) + circular on-screen numeric keypad with delete; auto-submits on the 4th digit. No system keyboard. Reused by the idle screen-lock.

components.name: ds-state-panel
components.notes: NET-NEW: centred blocking/empty state — icon circle + title + body + optional secondary action. Used by app-disabled, no-access, no-rooms, PIN-lockout, bootstrap-failed.

components.name: ds-lock-screen
components.notes: NET-NEW: idle auto-lock overlay — educator photo + 'Screen locked' + PIN re-auth (reuses ds-pin-keypad) + 'Not <name>?' / 'Log out'. Shared-device safeguard.

motion.notes: Launch splash ~1s on cold start (bouncing-dots loader); sign-in shows a ~1.15s loading state; the hero scene drifts and the bottom sheet slides between steps; lists stagger in and PIN dots pop; the hub idle auto-locks after ~30s. prefers-reduced-motion freezes scenes, disables stagger/pop, and removes the toggle slide.

screens.name: Service sign-in
screens.purpose: Authenticate the device to a childcare service.
screens.exit: → S2 Educator select on success; inline error on failure (see e-creds)
layout: Full-bleed teal hero (≈338px phone): drifting kid-friendly scene + Playground 'P' mark + 'Rise and shine' heading + subtitle
White sheet overlaps the hero (rounded top, ~24px overlap)
Service username field → Service password field (reveal toggle)
Sign in button (full-width, pinned to the bottom of the sheet)
Forgot password? link (centred, under the button)

copy.text: Sign in to your service and start your day.

redlines.spec: white, top radius ~28–32px, overlaps hero by ~24px
52px tall, full-width, radius-lg, teal action-primary, white text; pinned bottom

behaviour: Sign in is enabled only when both username and password are non-empty.
Tap Sign in → ~1.15s loading state (spinner + 'Signing in…') → success routes to S2.
Invalid credentials show the inline service error (e-creds) and keep the user on S1.
Password field has a trailing reveal toggle (show/hide).
Demo credentials in the prototype: username LittleBugs · password bugs123.
a11y: Both inputs have visible persistent labels; focus order top→bottom.
Password reveal toggle is a labelled button (aria-label 'Toggle password').
Inline error is announced (role=alert); error text contrast ≥4.5:1.

acceptance.text: Sign in is disabled until both fields have content.
Valid credentials show the loading state then route to S2.
Invalid credentials show the inline service error and stay on S1.
Password reveal toggle flips the field between masked and plain.

screens.name: Educator select
screens.purpose: Choose which educator is signing in to this service.
screens.exit: → S4 PIN on selecting an educator; → S3 Add educator on tapping add
layout: Teal hero (shrunk) — brand mark is now the SERVICE logo (e.g. Little Bugs crest); 'Log out' nav top-left
Educator list — photo avatar + name + 'Signed in <ago>'
Whole page scrolls (hero scrolls away; NOT sticky)

redlines.spec: card, radius-lg, 42px photo avatar + name + last-login; rows stagger in
single scroll container — the hero scrolls off; not pinned

behaviour: Selecting an educator carries it into context and routes to S4 PIN.
Search filters the list live; no match shows the empty line.
Sort toggles between most-recent-login and alphabetical.
Add-educator row opens S3.
Avatars are photo-first with an initials fallback when the image can't load.
a11y: Educator rows are buttons with accessible names (name + last-login); ≥44px targets.
Sort pills expose selected state; search field has a label.
Avatar images are decorative (the name carries identity).

acceptance.text: Selecting an educator routes to S4 carrying that educator.
A search with no matches shows the no-match line.
The add-educator row opens S3.

screens.name: Add educator profile
screens.purpose: Sign in a new educator profile not yet in the list.
screens.entry: From the add-educator row on S2
screens.exit: → S5 Room select on success; back returns to S2

copy.text: Please sign into your Educator Profile

redlines.spec: enabled only when both fields filled

behaviour: Sign in enabled only when both fields are non-empty.
Password/PIN field has a reveal toggle.
On success routes to S5 Room select.
a11y: Labelled inputs; reveal toggle is a labelled button; back nav is reachable.

acceptance.text: Sign in is disabled until both fields have content.
Successful add routes to S5.

screens.name: PIN entry
screens.purpose: Verify the selected educator with a 4-digit PIN.
screens.entry: After selecting an educator on S2
screens.exit: → S5 Room select (cold start) / → Room Hub (returning) on correct PIN; → S7 via 'Use password'; lockout after 5 failures (e-locked)
layout: Teal hero — brand mark is now the EDUCATOR'S PHOTO + greeting
4 PIN dots (ring → filled, pop on entry)
Circular on-screen numeric keypad with delete
behaviour: PIN auto-submits once the 4th digit is entered (no submit button).
Incorrect PIN shakes the dots and shows 'Incorrect PIN — <n> tries left'; attempts increment.
After 5 incorrect attempts the screen shows the locked panel (e-locked) with a Switch educator action.
'Forgot PIN? Use password' routes to S7 (password fallback).
Demo PIN in the prototype: 1234.
Back returns to the educator list (or same-day for returning users).
a11y: Keypad keys are ≥44px labelled buttons; delete key labelled.
PIN progress is announced; the incorrect message is announced (role=alert).
Don't rely on colour alone for the incorrect state (message + shake + remaining count).

acceptance.text: Entering the 4th digit auto-submits without a button.
A correct PIN advances to S5 (cold start).
Each incorrect PIN shows remaining tries and increments attempts.
acceptance.assert: submit(incorrect) -> message=='Incorrect PIN — '+(5-attempts)+' tries left' AND attempts++

acceptance.text: Five incorrect attempts lock the screen.
acceptance.assert: attempts>=5 -> e-locked panel visible AND keypad hidden

acceptance.text: The password link routes to the password fallback (S7).

screens.name: Room select
screens.purpose: Choose the room the educator is working in today.
screens.entry: After a correct PIN (cold start) or Add educator
layout: Room rows — photo thumbnail + name + ratio, selectable
Whole page scrolls; hero scrolls away

redlines.spec: selectable card; 46px photo thumbnail + name + 'Ratio x:1'; selected = teal border + green check
greyed, dashed border, status chip (e.g. 'At capacity'), not selectable
disabled until a room is selected; pinned bottom

behaviour: Selecting a room enables Continue and updates its label to 'Continue to <Room>'.
Continue routes to the Room Hub.
Needs-attention rooms (under ratio) are selectable; disabled rooms (closed / at capacity) are not.
A closed room shows a warning but can still be entered (see e-closed).
Room thumbnails are placeholder photos with a letter-tile fallback if the image can't load.
a11y: Room rows are radio-like selectable buttons (name + ratio + status); selected exposed non-visually; disabled rooms are not focusable as actions.

acceptance.text: Continue is disabled until a selectable room is chosen.
Continue routes to the Room Hub with the selected room in context.
Disabled rooms cannot be selected.

screens.name: Welcome back (same-day)
screens.purpose: Fast-path a returning educator to their remembered room — with shared-device escape hatches.
screens.exit: → PIN re-auth on Continue; Change room → S5; Switch educator → S2; Log out → S1
layout: Teal hero (educator photo) — 'Log out' nav top-left

redlines.spec: phone/iPad-portrait top-aligned under the hero; iPad-landscape centred

behaviour: Shows the room remembered from the last session as pre-selected.
Continue re-authenticates with PIN (S4) before entering the hub.
Change room opens the full S5 list.
'Switch educator' drops the remembered educator and returns to S2 (service stays signed in) — for when a different person picks up the tablet.
'Log out' signs the service out and returns to S1.
a11y: Greeting heading receives focus on entry; Change room / Switch educator / Log out are clearly labelled controls.

acceptance.text: The remembered room is pre-selected and Continue re-auths via PIN.
Change room opens the full room list (S5).
Switch educator returns to the educator list without logging the service out.

screens.name: Educator password (PIN fallback)
screens.purpose: Let an educator sign in with a password when they can't use their PIN.
screens.entry: From 'Forgot PIN? Use password' on S4
screens.exit: → S5 Room select (cold start) / → Room Hub (returning) on success; 'Use PIN instead' → S4

redlines.spec: disabled until the password field has content

behaviour: Sign in enabled only when the password field is non-empty.
On success routes onward exactly like a correct PIN (S5 cold start / hub returning).
'Use PIN instead' returns to S4.
a11y: Labelled input; reveal toggle is a labelled button; both links reachable.

acceptance.text: Sign in is disabled until the password has content.
Success routes onward like a correct PIN.
'Use PIN instead' returns to S4.

stateMatrix.ui: inline error on the password field
stateMatrix.copy: We couldn't sign in to that service, please try again. For password reset please contact the service administrator.
This app has been disabled for your service. Please contact your service administrator.

stateMatrix.trigger: Account lacks access to the app
stateMatrix.copy: Your account doesn't have access to this app. Contact your service administrator to request access.
Something went wrong loading the educator list. Check your connection and try again.
Too many incorrect attempts for <FirstName>. Try again in 5 minutes, or switch educator.

stateMatrix.trigger: No rooms set up for the service
stateMatrix.copy: There are no rooms set up for this service yet. Contact your service administrator.
<Room> is closed today. You can still enter if you need to.

stateMatrix.trigger: Educator uses password auth instead of PIN

stateMatrix.copy: Something went wrong while loading. Please try again.

stateMatrix.trigger: Tablet idle ~30s on the hub (shared device)
stateMatrix.ui: full lock overlay — educator photo + PIN re-auth
stateMatrix.copy: Screen locked — Enter <FirstName>'s PIN to continue

platformDeltas.android: Jetpack Navigation Compose; equivalent back stack
resources / Compose theme from the same token values

platformDeltas.ios: Custom on-screen circular keypad (no system keyboard)
platformDeltas.android: Custom on-screen circular keypad (no system keyboard)

platformDeltas.ios: App-level inactivity timer (~30s on hub) → lock overlay; reset on touch
platformDeltas.android: Equivalent inactivity timer → lock overlay; reset on touch

platformDeltas.ios: Async image load (educator photos / room thumbnails) with initials/letter fallback
platformDeltas.android: Coil/async load with the same fallback

platformDeltas.ios: iPad landscape uses a hero/form split + centred return screen
iOS 17+ assumed — iOS 16 support is an OPEN question (~2,000 field devices below 17)

openQuestions.q: Idle auto-lock timeout — prototype uses ~30s on the hub. Confirm the production value and whether it also applies to room-select / same-day, plus whether biometric (Face/Touch ID) is an allowed unlock alongside PIN.
PIN lockout = 5 attempts in the prototype, with a '5 minute' cool-down copy. Confirm the real threshold, cool-down duration, and admin reset path.
Confirm iOS minimum deployment target — iOS 17+ vs iOS 16 support for ~2,000 field devices (affects SwiftUI APIs).
Room Hub (Phase 3) — this flow routes into it but the hub itself is a placeholder (roster skeleton). Needs its own spec.

decisions.rationale: Chosen from the six-direction comparison; full-bleed teal hero + white sheet, warm/kid-friendly for an OSHC/childcare audience, on Stardust tokens.

decisions.decision: Treat the device as a SHARED room tablet.
decisions.rationale: Multiple educators per device → Switch educator + Log out are always reachable, and the hub idle auto-locks so a left-open tablet can't be used as the signed-in educator.

decisions.decision: Brand mark swaps with progress (Playground → service logo → educator photo).
decisions.rationale: Reinforces who is signing in and into which service at each step.

decisions.decision: PIN lockout at 5 attempts; password is an always-available fallback (S7).
decisions.rationale: Recovers a forgotten/locked PIN without a dead-end; resolves the earlier open lockout question (pending final threshold confirmation).

changelog.change: Isolated the Playful tall-scene as the chosen direction (v0.1): placeholder images + brand logo-swap, redesigned PIN, non-sticky hero scroll.

Shared-device safety (Switch educator + idle auto-lock); PIN 'tries left' + lockout-after-5 + password fallback (S7); iPad parity (photos, room thumbnails, circular keypad); image fallbacks; keyboard focus rings; sliding tab-toggle; handoff retargeted to the v0.2 tall-scene prototype.
